title: Angle of refraction
short: The angle between a refracted ray and the normal after it crosses into a new medium, in degrees.
full:
The angle of refraction θ₂ is the angle, measured from the normal, at which a ray travels after bending as it enters a new medium. Light entering a denser medium (higher n) bends toward the normal, so θ₂ is smaller than θ₁; entering a less dense medium it bends away.

Snell's law gives it: n₁·sin θ₁ = n₂·sin θ₂. If the required sine exceeds 1 there is no refracted ray — the light is totally internally reflected.

It is measured in degrees (or radians).
